skill.name: seo-fundamentals
skill.description: Explains core SEO principles, including E-E-A-T, Core Web Vitals, technical foundations, content quality, and page evaluation signals.
skill.summary: Learn the fundamentals behind sustainable search visibility and page quality.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives caused by SEO terminology such as description, meta description, HTTPS, and page-experience text. The included script reads local HTML, JSX, and TSX page files for SEO checks, but no evidence of malicious intent, exfiltration, network access, external command execution, environment access, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SEO Text Matched Weak Crypto Pattern
low_findings.description: The static findings are dismissed. The flagged lines contain SEO documentation and string checks for description, meta description, HTTP, HTTPS, and mobile page experience terms, not weak cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is ordinary SEO terminology, and there is no cryptographic library, hash function, cipher selection, or password handling nearby. Confidence that this is a real security issue is very low.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File Reads for SEO Audit
low_findings.description: The script accepts a project path, searches for public page files, and reads matching files to report SEO issues. This filesystem access is expected for the skill purpose and does not send data outside the local process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is directly present and user-controlled by the project path. The risk remains low because the script has no network calls, external commands, or secret handling.

content.user_title: Learn SEO Fundamentals for Better Pages
content.value_statement: Teams often optimize pages without understanding how search engines evaluate quality, trust, and accessibility. This skill explains durable SEO principles and helps review pages against practical fundamentals.
actual_capabilities: Explains E-E-A-T as a quality evaluation framework.
Summarizes Core Web Vitals and how they affect page experience.
Reviews technical SEO foundations such as sitemaps, robots.txt, canonical tags, and status codes.
Identifies common page-level content signals such as titles, headings, descriptions, and alt text.
Includes a local Python checker for basic HTML, JSX, and TSX page SEO issues.
limitations: Does not guarantee search ranking changes.
Does not replace professional SEO strategy or analytics review.
The checker covers basic on-page signals, not full site crawling.
Search guidance may need updates as search engine documentation changes.

use_cases.title: Review a Launch Page
use_cases.description: Check whether a new page has clear titles, descriptions, headings, and accessible images before release.

use_cases.title: Teach SEO Basics
use_cases.description: Explain search quality concepts to writers, developers, or stakeholders using direct foundational guidance.

use_cases.title: Audit Frontend Pages
use_cases.description: Run a lightweight local check across page files to find missing metadata and simple structure issues.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the SEO fundamentals I should understand before improving a website. Focus on content quality, trust, technical access, and user experience.

prompt_templates.title: Review Page Signals
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for SEO fundamentals. Identify issues with the title, meta description, headings, image alt text, structured data, and trust signals.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Improvements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized SEO improvement plan for these page findings. Separate technical fixes, content quality work, and measurement tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitive Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this page against competing pages using E-E-A-T, search intent coverage, Core Web Vitals, structure, originality, and trust signals.

output_examples.input: Review a product landing page for SEO fundamentals.
output: The page has a clear primary topic, but the title should include the product category.
The meta description should explain the user benefit and match search intent.
The page needs stronger trust signals, such as author details, support information, or customer proof.

output_examples.input: Explain why Core Web Vitals matter.
output_examples.output: Core Web Vitals measure loading speed, interactivity, and visual stability. They usually support strong content rather than replace it.

output_examples.input: Prioritize SEO fixes after a basic audit.
output: Fix missing titles and descriptions on public pages.
Resolve multiple H1 headings where they confuse page structure.
Add useful alt text to informative images.

best_practices: Start with search intent and content usefulness before metadata changes.
Validate technical access so search engines can crawl and understand pages.
Use multiple measurement signals instead of relying on one metric.
anti_patterns: Publishing unreviewed AI-generated content without original value.
Chasing keyword density instead of answering the query clearly.
Treating structured data as a ranking shortcut.

faq.question: Does this skill perform a complete SEO audit?
faq.answer: No. It teaches fundamentals and includes a basic local checker for common page issues.

faq.question: Can it improve rankings automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and checks, but ranking depends on many external and competitive factors.

faq.question: What files can the checker inspect?
faq.answer: The checker looks for HTML, HTM, JSX, and TSX files that appear to be public pages.

faq.question: Does the checker send page content anywhere?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed script reads local files and prints local results.

faq.question: Is E-E-A-T a direct ranking factor?
faq.answer: No. The skill presents it as a quality evaluation framework, not a direct ranking factor.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Writers, marketers, developers, and site owners can use it to understand durable SEO basics.
